revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okunoyama Kofun | native_name =奥の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okunoyama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|22.4|N|139|28|37.59|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Okunoyama Kofun'''|奥の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[Sue ware]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest burial mound towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/okuno.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/奥の山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gyōda]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Sakitama Kofun Cluster]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okunoyama Kofun | native_name =奥の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okunoyama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|22.4|N|139|28|37.59|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Okunoyama Kofun'''|奥の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[Sue ware]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest burial mound towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[Sue ware]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest burial mound towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okunoyama Kofun | native_name =奥の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okunoyama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|22.4|N|139|28|37.59|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Okunoyama Kofun'''|奥の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[Sue ware]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest burial mound towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okunoyama Kofun | native_name =奥の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okunoyama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|22.4|N|139|28|37.59|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Okunoyama Kofun'''|奥の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[Sue ware]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest burial mound towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[Sue ware]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[Sue ware]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest burial mound towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.

==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[Sue ware]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Okunoyama Kofun | native_name =奥の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okunoyama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|22.4|N|139|28|37.59|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Okunoyama Kofun'''|奥の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[Sue pottery]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/okuno.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/奥の山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gyōda]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Okunoyama Kofun | native_name =奥の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okunoyama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|22.4|N|139|28|37.59|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Okunoyama Kofun'''|奥の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳|12=draft}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[Sue pottery]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[:en:Inariyama Kofun|Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunyama Kofun|qq=|1=Shōgun'yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458633}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okunoyama Kofun]][[en:Okunoyama Kofun]][[ja:奥の山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11445573]] Wikidata name: Okunoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Okunoyama Kofun | native_name =奥の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Okunoyama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.122888888889|N|139.47710833333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Okunoyama Kofun'''|奥の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[:en:Inariyama Kofun|Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunyama Kofun|1=Shōgun'yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458633}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

[[en:Okunoyama Kofun]]
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Okunoyama Kofun}} [[ja:奥の山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11445573}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okunoyama Kofun]][[en:Okunoyama Kofun]][[ja:奥の山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11445573]] Wikidata name: Okunoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Okunoyama Kofun | native_name =奥の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Okunoyama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.122888888889|N|139.47710833333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Okunoyama Kofun'''|奥の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[:en:Inariyama Kofun|Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunyama Kofun|1=Shōgun'yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458633}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Okunoyama Kofun'''|奥の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the [[:en:Inariyama Kofun|Inariyama Kofun]], [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunyama Kofun|1=Shōgun'yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458633}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high

Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Okunoyama Kofun | native_name =奥の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Okunoyama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.122888888889|N|139.47710833333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Okunoyama Kofun'''|奥の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|en|Inariyama Kofun|WD=Q3198272|lt=Inariyama Kofun}}, [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunyama Kofun|1=Shōgun'yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458633}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|WD=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/okuno.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/奥の山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns] {{in lang|ja}}

These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|en|Inariyama Kofun|WD=Q3198272|lt=Inariyama Kofun}}, [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunyama Kofun|1=Shōgun'yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458633}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|en|Inariyama Kofun|WD=Q3198272|lt=Inariyama Kofun|1=Inariyama Kofun}}, [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunyama Kofun|1=Shōgun'yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458633}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Okunoyama Kofun | native_name =奥の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Okunoyama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okunoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.122888888889|N|139.47710833333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Okunoyama Kofun'''|奥の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okunoyama Kofun has a total length of 70 meters, and is thus the second smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Okunoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat; however, part of the outer moat overlaps that of the adjacent [[Teppōyama Kofun]], which was built slightly later. It is thought to have been built in the middle of the 6th century AD, based on ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the inner moat in 1968. These included cylindrical, horse-shaped, equestrian statue-shaped, and shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa''.
In an excavation of the main tumulus in 2018, it was found that the middle bank in the southwest corner protrudes partly to the outside. This is a feature which also appears in the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|en|Inariyama Kofun|WD=Q3198272|lt=Inariyama Kofun|1=Inariyama Kofun}}, [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Shogunyama Kofun|ja|将軍山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunyama Kofun|1=Shōgun'yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458633}} in the Sakitama Kofun Group, but is rarely found in other locations. Called a "Tsukuridashi", the shield-bearer shaped ''haniwa'' were also found lined up on the southwestern corner, indicating that this location was of some especial ritual significance. Also of note was a decorated {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|WD=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/okuno.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/奥の山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

Also of note was a decorated {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jar, with a round-bottom and long-neck, together with three smaller jars of the same shape. These are thought to have been placed on an offering stand that was also excavated. There are very few examples of such Sue jars excavated in the Kantō region, and the fact that it was excavated from the second smallest Kofun towards the back of the cluster indicate that this tumulus had an importance greater than is reflected by its relatively small size.
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 6.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 7.4 meters high
